sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Milan are in excellent form, unbeaten in their last 10 matches with strong performances, including a recent 1-0 win over Roma. Parma, on the other hand, are struggling, winless in their last 5 games, with defensive frailties evident in recent losses like the 1-3 defeat to Bologna.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Parma and Milan have been high-scoring, with both teams scoring in each game. Milan edged out a 3-2 victory in their most recent clash, while Parma secured a 2-1 win at home earlier this season.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Milan, sitting 3rd in the league with 21 points, boast a strong attack and solid defense, conceding just 7 goals this season. Parma, languishing in 17th with only 7 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 5 goals, the lowest in the league, and showing poor ball possession at 41.4%.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Milan, Luka Modrić (7.73 average rating) and Christian Pulišić (4 goals in last 5) are in top form, posing a significant threat. Parma will rely on goalkeeper Zion Suzuki (7.62 rating in last 5) to keep Milan’s attack at bay.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Milan’s ability to score early (often first in 8 of 10 recent games) and their dominance in final-third play could exploit Parma’s defensive lapses. Parma’s strength in aerial duels (51.6% won) might offer some counter-attacking opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Milan’s superior form, league standing, and attacking prowess, they are heavily favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 74% probability for an away victory. Parma’s poor recent results and low goal output make an upset unlikely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Parma and Milan at the Ennio Tardini saw an engaging battle with Milan leading 2-1 at the break. Milan struck early with a goal from Alexis Saelemaekers, capitalizing on a promising move in the opening minutes. They doubled their lead through a penalty converted by Rafael Leão midway through the half. Parma fought back late with a goal from Adrián Bernabé just before halftime, giving them a lifeline heading into the second half. Both teams showed equal ball possession, indicating a balanced contest despite the scoreline favoring Milan.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The attack momentum in the first half swung back and forth between the two sides. Milan started strongly with consistent attacking pressure in the initial minutes, leading to their opening goal. Parma responded with a spell of dominance, pushing forward with intent in the following phase. However, Milan regained control during a key period, which resulted in their second goal. Towards the end of the half, Parma mounted a significant push, culminating in their goal just before the whistle. The momentum shifts suggest both teams are capable of creating dangerous moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Milan showed a slightly more clinical edge in the first half, converting their opportunities with a higher expected goals tally compared to Parma. They also demonstrated strength in duels, winning a higher percentage both on the ground and in the air. Parma, despite being behind, managed to create entries into the final third and penalty area, showing they can penetrate Milan's defense. However, their lack of big chances created indicates a need for more incisiveness in attack. Defensively, both teams were relatively evenly matched, with neither side conceding many clear-cut opportunities beyond the goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Milan, Alexis Saelemaekers stood out with an impressive performance, scoring the opener and contributing defensively with interceptions. Rafael Leão also made his mark with a confidently taken penalty to secure their lead. On Parma's side, Adrián Bernabé was the standout, not only for his late goal but also for his overall influence in midfield, reflected in his high rating. Sascha Britschgi provided a crucial assist for Parma, showing creativity in the final third. These players will likely be central to their teams' efforts in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be a tight affair as Parma will look to build on their late first-half momentum to level the score. Milan, on the other hand, will aim to maintain their lead by exploiting counter-attacking opportunities, given their clinical finishing so far. Parma's ability to convert their final third entries into tangible chances will be key if they are to mount a comeback. Milan’s superior duel-winning stats might give them an edge in maintaining control, but they must be wary of Parma’s desperation to equalize. Expect both teams to push hard, with tactical adjustments likely playing a significant role.
